seo.title: Pricing
seo.description: Review Geek Engine pricing for solo creators and studios, including credits, collaboration seats, storage, and production add-ons.

hero.heading: Predictable, resource-backed plans.
hero.sub: Every tier features transparent credit mapping. {credits} workspace credits converts directly to {value} of raw background processing and AI consumption value — keeping your costs entirely open.

hobbyist.name: Hobbyist
features: Web-only publishing via hosted platform player
Client-side local storage for default game saves
Basic asset library uploads and generation features

core.name: Core Creator
features: Web publishing with white-label & custom domain support

studio.name: Studio Pro
features: Direct cross-platform commercial export to Steam and Mobile stores
Turbo priority cloud build queue (isolated worker instances)
Advanced 3D model parsing and animation pipeline matching
Full raw project source-code zip extraction and downloading

addOns.label: Add-ons
addOns.heading: Scale your studio workspace on demand.

seat.name: Additional seat licensing
seat.body: Available across all subscription plans. Adding an extra user seat to your organization instantly unlocks advanced collaboration pathways and injects an additional {credits} creation credits into your team's shared monthly workspace balance.

credits.name: Metered credit overage top-ups
credits.body: Keep your design pipeline active. Purchase extra processing capacity whenever your studio hits peak development cycles.

database.name: Live cloud database add-on
database.body: Unlock online persistence. The platform automatically provisions a secure, cloud-hosted relational database for your game. The AI automatically generates the remote network logic scripts required to run live multiplayer leaderboards, secure player accounts, and cloud saves.

items.q: How are credits calculated during production?
items.a: Our credit system maps exactly to the background processing cost of your changes. Minor text tweaks or node reorder tasks cost minimal credits, whereas generating deep 3D assets or running intensive self-healing error analysis loops burns higher volumes of your allocated pool.

items.q: Can teams pool their credits together?
items.a: Yes. All credits included in your base plan, alongside any credits added by purchasing extra user seats, are placed into a centralized pool accessible by all authorized editors within that specific organization workspace.

items.q: What happens if our project does not buy the cloud database?
items.a: By default, your games utilize client-side local storage frameworks. This follows standard platform rules for single-player games, offline settings saving, and local machine progress. You only require the database add-on if you want your game to read and write records across a persistent global web network.

ctaStrip.heading: Pricing is published. Access is coming soon.
ctaStrip.sub: Join the list and we will send the first availability window.
